meta.name: Japanese N5
meta.description: Core N5 grammar particles and verb forms — the foundation of Japanese sentence structure.
meta.author: concept

nodes.title: は (wa)
nodes.content_data: Topic marker — marks the sentence topic. Does not mark the grammatical subject.

nodes.title: が (ga)
nodes.content_data: Subject marker — marks the grammatical subject. Emphasises who does the action.

nodes.title: を (wo)
nodes.content_data: Object marker — marks the direct object of a verb.

nodes.title: で (de)
nodes.content_data: Location of action / means — 'at' (where action happens) or 'by means of'.

nodes.title: ます-form
nodes.content_data: Polite present/future verb form. Used in formal speech and writing. e.g. 食べます (tabemasu).

nodes.title: て-form
nodes.content_data: Connects actions, forms requests (〜てください), permission (〜てもいい), and compound verbs. e.g. 食べて (tabete).

nodes.title: ない-form
nodes.content_data: Plain negative form. Negates verbs in casual speech. e.g. 食べない (tabenai).

nodes.title: です (desu)
nodes.content_data: Polite copula — links subject to noun or adjective predicate. Equivalent of 'is/am/are'.

nodes.title: だ (da)
nodes.content_data: Plain copula — casual equivalent of です. Used in plain-form sentences.
